clusters.title: Supreme Court Upholds State Bans on Transgender Athletes in Sports
clusters.summary: The US Supreme Court has upheld state laws that prohibit transgender women and girls from competing in female sports, allowing states to enforce these bans. The ruling has significant implications for LGBTQ rights and the participation of transgender individuals in school and college athletics.

items.title: US supreme court rules states can exclude trans athletes from female sports
items.description:
Justices voted to overturn judgments issued by lower courts in favor of trans students who sued after being barred from competing in West Virginia and Idaho

  • Supreme court decisions – live updates

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

The US supreme court has upheld laws in two conservative states excluding transgender girls and women from competing in female sports in a far-reaching ruling certain to impact trans rights throughout the US.

The court’s nine justices voted to overturn previous judgements issued by lower courts in favor of two trans students who had sued after being barred from competing in West Virginia and Idaho respectively.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Allows Trump Administration's Immigration Policy Revival
timeline_highlights.summary: The Supreme Court has cleared the way for the Trump administration to revive a controversial immigration detention policy that affects asylum seekers. This decision enables the administration to implement stricter measures regarding immigration, which has sparked debate over its implications for asylum rights and border control. The ruling is significant as it reflects ongoing tensions surrounding immigration policy in the United States.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Rules to Count Late Mail-in Ballots Postmarked Before Election Day
timeline_highlights.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that states may count mail-in ballots arriving after Election Day, provided they are postmarked by that date. This decision came in response to a challenge from the Republican National Committee, which sought to prevent the counting of such ballots. The ruling is significant as it impacts voting procedures and the handling of ballots in upcoming elections.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Denies Trump's Appeal in E. Jean Carroll Case
timeline_highlights.summary: The US Supreme Court has declined to hear President Trump's appeal to overturn a jury's verdict that found he sexually abused and defamed writer E. Jean Carroll, resulting in a $5 million award. This decision marks another setback for Trump in his ongoing legal battles related to the case, which he has consistently denied. The ruling underscores the court's stance on the jury's findings in this high-profile civil matter.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Rules Trump Cannot Fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook
timeline_highlights.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that Trump cannot remove Lisa Cook from her position as a Federal Reserve governor, marking a significant decision regarding presidential authority over agency leaders. This ruling comes amid a broader context in which the Court has expanded presidential powers to dismiss certain agency officials. The decision underscores the unique status of the Federal Reserve in the federal government structure.

clusters.title: Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship, Rejects Trump Limits
clusters.summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the principle of birthright citizenship, rejecting attempts to impose limits proposed by Donald Trump. This decision has sparked celebrations among supporters outside the court, highlighting the significance of citizenship rights in the ongoing national debate. The ruling reinforces the legal framework that grants citizenship to individuals born on U.S. soil, regardless of their parents' immigration status.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship, Rejects Trump Limits
timeline_highlights.summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the principle of birthright citizenship, rejecting attempts to impose limits proposed by Donald Trump. This decision has sparked celebrations among supporters outside the court, highlighting the significance of citizenship rights in the ongoing national debate. The ruling reinforces the legal framework that grants citizenship to individuals born on U.S. soil, regardless of their parents' immigration status.

timeline_highlights.title: Supreme Court Eliminates Limits on Party Campaign Spending

The ruling has significant implications for the financing of political campaigns in the United States.

The disaster has intensified calls for improved emergency preparedness and infrastructure in the region.

events.title: Prosecutor Held in Contempt in Charlie Kirk Murder Case
events.summary: Trump expressed his indecision regarding a significant housing bill, labeling it 'a yawn' and suggesting it lacks urgency. He also acknowledged that the SAVE America Act is unlikely to pass, while continuing to advocate for measures to curb voting access. This commentary reflects ongoing debates about housing policy and electoral reforms.

timeline.title: Judge Blocks Trump's Order to Limit Mail Voting
timeline.summary: A judge has blocked an order that would have required the Postal Service to impose restrictions on mail voting. The ruling comes amid ongoing debates about voting access and election integrity. This decision is significant as it impacts the ability of voters to utilize mail-in ballots in upcoming elections.

timeline.title: Prosecutor Held in Contempt in Charlie Kirk Murder Case
timeline.summary: A judge has held a prosecutor in contempt for making public comments regarding the defendant in the Charlie Kirk murder case. Despite this ruling, the judge has indicated that the death penalty remains a potential option in the proceedings. This development underscores ongoing tensions in the high-profile case.
